page: Microsoft Azure FX48ms_v2 is a memory-intensive server instance belonging to the FXmsv2 family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The configuration delivers 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 4.2 GHz. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for integer and tile operations.

The instance is equipped with 1008.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per core. This massive memory capacity is balanced by the complete absence of local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external Premium Storage options. There are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance type.

In benchmark evaluations, the FX48ms_v2 demonstrates strong memory bandwidth, reaching 4139.14 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 331.05 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Single-core CPU performance is notable, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2357.0 and strong image and text processing capabilities. Multi-threaded performance is also robust, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU score of 70644.51 and a Redis SET throughput of 76.26 million operations per second.

Given its high-frequency dedicated CPU cores and substantial memory footprint, the FX48ms_v2 offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads. It is suited for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching systems like Redis, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference, where its AMX instructions accelerate matrix operations. The lack of local storage requires external storage provisioning, which should be factored into the overall architectural cost.
description: Microsoft Azure FX48ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the FXmsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C x86_64 processor with 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) operating at 4.2 GHz, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache. The instance provides 1008.0 GB of RAM, translating to 21.0 GB per core, but includes no local storage or bundled GPUs. Benchmarks indicate strong memory bandwidth, with uncached large-block reads at 331.05 GB/sec, and top-tier single-core performance in image and text processing. The processor's support for AVX-512 and AMX instructions enhances its capability for mathematical and cryptographic operations. This server is designed for memory-heavy and compute-intensive workloads such as large database operations, in-memory caching, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX48ms_v2 is a memory-intensive server featuring 48 vCPUs, 1008 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, designed for large databases and in-memory caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX48ms_v2 features 48 vCPUs, 1008 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-intensive virtual server featuring one terabyte of RAM and advanced matrix extensions for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 48 vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz
1008.0 GB of system memory providing 21.0 GB per core
No bundled local storage, requiring external Premium Storage solutions
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix and vector math
Strong memory bandwidth and top-tier single-core processing benchmarks
Suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and CPU-based inference
